concept: Progress / Move Toward a Stated Work Criterion
description: To move toward a stated stage or completion criterion. Progress is always relative to that criterion: it can pause, reverse, or become irrelevant when the plan changes, and change alone is not progress. The word carries no moral improvement and no inevitability.
sound_symbolism: The opening 'wepu' contour recalls going without copying the shorter verb whole, and final 'ri' keeps practical effort in motion. The sound moves forward but does not close in triumph, fitting progress that remains conditional and measured against a stated destination.
grammatical_notes: An intransitive verb: 'ha rinoka wepuri.' (This project progresses.) State the relevant stage or criterion with surrounding clauses when it is not shared. The event-noun rule supplies progress as the observed movement. Distinct from 'sholu' (complete), 'poruli' (efficient), 'thuroa' (grow), and 'wepu' (go).

pillars.solarpunk-values: Progress can be evaluated against repair, access, ecological effect, and human need instead of being assumed from growth or activity alone.
pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: A relative progress word keeps attention on changing conditions and chosen criteria rather than treating improvement as an inherent property of time.

tags.activity: movement toward a stated practical criterion
tags.cognition: an interpreted relation between current and intended states
tags.temporal: change across stages of an undertaking
